title: Fast shuts down, board culpability, Elon buys 9% of Twitter, deplatforming's evolution & more
description:
0:00 Jason's big night out, bestie intros, All-In Summit update and more

8:01 Layoffs and shutdowns: Fast, Better.com, GoPuff; Chamath gives a macro- and micro- overview for startups

14:09 Preventing layoffs, culpability in Fast's shutdown, VC diligence strategy, VC/founder model

41:51 Elon buys a 9% stake in Twitter and joins the board: what does this mean for Twitter, free speech online, evolution of deplatforming

1:03:24 Food shortage update, ideal US objectives going forward

analysis.summary: The episode discusses recent startup shutdowns, Elon Musk's Twitter stake, and the evolution of deplatforming. The hosts also touch on food shortages and US objectives. Chamath provides a macro- and micro- overview for startups, while the hosts debate board culpability in Fast's shutdown.

takeaways.text: Elon Musk's Twitter stake may impact free speech online
Deplatforming is evolving, with platforms taking a more active role
Food shortages are a growing concern, with US corn stocks expected to drop
US objectives should focus on addressing food shortages and promoting stability
The VC/founder model is being reevaluated in light of recent startup failures
Boards have a responsibility to prevent startup failures

sentiment.notes: The hosts express concern about the current state of startups and the potential impact of Elon Musk's Twitter stake on free speech. They also discuss the need for boards to take responsibility for preventing startup failures.

notable_moments.description: Elon Musk buys a 9% stake in Twitter
Discussion of food shortages and US objectives
